message:
refactor(runtime): retire `not(feature = "asupersync-runtime")` legacy-tokio test/bench fallbacks (ft-nm5nc)

Follow-up to ft-f6vhg's verification (which falsified the "no-op
flag" premise) and ft-g43fq's runtime_compat → runtime_async rename:
the legacy-tokio fallback bodies these gates wrapped have been dead
since ft-xbnl0.2.5 made asupersync the sole async runtime. The
`asupersync-runtime` feature is in default-features, the canonical
async surface is now `runtime_async`, and the API shapes the
`not(...)` branches called against (e.g. `tx.reserve()` with no
`&Cx`) no longer exist on the wrapper layer.

7 sites updated:

* tests/proptest_runtime_compat.rs:188-198 — paired
  cfg(feature = ...) / cfg(not(...)) blocks around mpsc::SendError
  shape assertions. Kept only the asupersync-shape branch
  (`SendError::Disconnected(value)`); the legacy `send_err.0`
  branch is gone.

* tests/robot_sdk_transport_integration.rs:33-49 —
  send_shutdown helper had two branches: asupersync's
  `shutdown_tx.reserve(&cx).await` path and a legacy
  `shutdown_tx.send(()).await` path. Kept only the asupersync
  branch.

* benches/tailer.rs:210-221 — bench_capture_event_channel had
  the same paired pattern. Collapsed to the asupersync `&cx`
  branch.

* tests/runtime_compat_tests.rs — entire file was
  `#![cfg(not(feature = "asupersync-runtime"))]`, testing the
  legacy "tokio path" of the dual-runtime compatibility layer.
  Disabled wholesale via `#![cfg(any())]` (RULE 1: file
  preserved, not deleted) with a header explaining the
  asupersync-side equivalents are covered by
  proptest_runtime_compat / pool_labruntime / lab_smoke / the
  *_labruntime suite.

* tests/replay_labruntime.rs:7-9 — doc comment describing the
  retired gate. Updated to past-tense, points at this bead.

* tests/ft_xbnl0_2_4_no_direct_tokio_net_or_rustls.rs:103-104 —
  doc comment about the legacy branch. Updated to past-tense,
  points at this bead.

* src/watchdog.rs:831-836 — doc comment on
  `get_mux_server_rss` (the ambient legacy variant alongside
  `get_mux_server_rss_with_cx`) referenced the now-retired
  fallback. Updated to past-tense and noted the function is
  kept as `#[allow(dead_code)]` until a follow-up cleanup
  confirms no out-of-tree caller still depends on it.

Verified via:

  rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cc6-target \
    CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang \
    CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \
    cargo check --workspace --all-targets

  + cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features mcp,asupersync-runtime \
      --test proptest_runtime_compat -- mpsc_send_to_closed_returns_value

cargo check clean. The edited mpsc_send_to_closed_returns_value
test passes. (An unrelated `watch_send_to_no_receivers_fails`
test in proptest_runtime_compat fails on main HEAD pre this
commit — verified by stashing my changes and re-running. Out of
scope.)

Per the bead's instruction: "file follow-up if any cfg gate
genuinely guards live tokio code." None of the 7 sites guarded
live tokio code; all were dead-code fallback branches. No
follow-up bead needed for that condition. The asupersync-runtime
feature flag itself stays — its retirement is the parent bead
ft-f6vhg's stalled work, blocked on the 50 file-level
`#![cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")]` gates which are out of
scope here. Did not touch codec/ or frankenterm/client/ (cc7
lane).

files.patch:
@@ -831,9 +831,12 @@ pub fn spawn_mux_watchdog(
 /// Get the RSS (resident set size) of the wezterm-mux-server process.
 ///
 /// Legacy ambient path preserved alongside the cx-first sibling
-/// `get_mux_server_rss_with_cx` used by the cx-first `check_inner`
-/// watchdog body. This ambient version is only invoked in the
-/// `#[cfg(not(feature = "asupersync-runtime"))]` fallback.
+/// [`get_mux_server_rss_with_cx`] used by the cx-first `check_inner`
+/// watchdog body. The historical `#[cfg(not(feature = "asupersync-runtime"))]`
+/// fallback that invoked this ambient variant was retired in ft-nm5nc
+/// (asupersync is the sole runtime); the function is kept as
+/// `#[allow(dead_code)]` until a follow-up cleanup confirms no
+/// out-of-tree caller still depends on it.
 #[allow(dead_code)]
 async fn get_mux_server_rss() -> Option<u64> {
     crate::runtime_compat::spawn_blocking(get_mux_server_rss_sync)

@@ -1,4 +1,15 @@
-#![cfg(not(feature = "asupersync-runtime"))]
+// ft-nm5nc: this entire file tested the legacy "tokio path" of the
+// dual-runtime compatibility layer. ft-xbnl0.2.5 made asupersync the
+// sole async runtime (the dual-runtime tokio fallback was removed),
+// and ft-g43fq renamed runtime_compat → runtime_async to reflect that
+// reality. Every test below was written against tokio-shape APIs
+// (e.g. `tx.reserve()` without a `&cx`) that no longer exist on the
+// canonical surface — they would fail to compile against today's
+// asupersync wrappers. Disabled wholesale via cfg(any()) rather than
+// deleted (AGENTS.md RULE 1); content preserved for audit. The
+// asupersync-side equivalents are covered by proptest_runtime_compat,
+// pool_labruntime, lab_smoke, and the surrounding *_labruntime suite.
+#![cfg(any())]
 
 //! Tests for runtime_compat module — dual-runtime compatibility layer.
 //!
